dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a cardiac arrest.
Hi, I'm EMT Brad. We are en route for a report of a cardiac arrest. Can you confirm the location?
Confirmed. Patient is in cardiac arrest. No spontaneous breathing or pulse upon arrival.
Eyes do not open. No verbal or motor response. GCS is 3.
Airway obstructed with mucus. Suctioned twice. A tooth was removed after dislodged by RFD firefighter.
Oropharyngeal airway placed to maintain airway patency.
No spontaneous breaths. Assisted ventilation with BVM initiated.
No central or peripheral pulse detected. Circulation absent.
Known history of anxiety, bipolar disorder, opioid abuse, and opioid dependency in remission.
Medications include Metoprolol, Cymbalta, clonidine, and Seroquel.
Skin is pale, cyanotic, and warm. No visible signs of trauma.
Head structures normal. No JVD. No visible airway or laryngeal trauma.
Equal chest rise when ventilating with BVM. No rise after initial intubation. Soft-tissue airway later compromised.
Abdomen soft and non-tender. No palpable masses or rigidity.
Multiple injection sites on arms. Injection marks noted on left forearm and right foot.
Oxygen saturation is 0% via pulse oximetry.
No pulse detected at initial and subsequent assessments.
Respirations at 0 per minute. No chest rise observed.
Blood pressure is 0/0 mmHg at initial and subsequent checks.
Initial glucose 36 mg/dL. Rechecked to 76 mg/dL upon ED arrival.
EKG with four lead monitoring initiated. No organized cardiac activity observed.
Epinephrine 1:10,000 administered six times via intraosseously; no response to interventions.
Manual CPR transitioned to Autopulse CPR.
Failed IV attempt. Intraosseous access established using 44 mm.
Narcan (2 mg) delivered intraosseously; no clinical improvement noted.
Oral intubation attempted. Tube misaligned in esophagus. Extubated and alternate airway initiated.
King airway placed but dislodged accidentally. NPA inserted for airway support.
Extubated due to esophageal tube misplacement.
Size 26 NPA in place. Equal chest rise observed bilaterally.
Airway suctioned for mucus and blood.
Dextrose 10% administered via intraosseous; no response observed.
Epinephrine re-administered via IO at 17:24. No improvement noted.
Protocol 2-7 activated for cardiac arrest care.
Patient secured on Reeves stretcher with 5-point harness before ambulance transfer.
Transporting to VCU Healthcare for ED team support.
Intraosseous line active. Interventions including Narcan, Epinephrine, and dextrose reported.
Care summary shared with Code Blue team upon ED arrival.
No pain reported per initial and ongoing assessment.
Pain level 0 per protocol and field notes.
